hypothesis_set.HFD-A: NEW LIVING AXIS — family-decoupling is axis-orthogonal new dimension; 4-axis greedy basis (own#3 c) revision required
hypothesis_set.HFD-B: SUB-AXIS OF CMT — family-decoupling is backbone-conditional variant of axis 95 CMT; sub-property of CMT finding
hypothesis_set.HFD-C: ARTIFACT — family-decoupling is artifact of layer count difference (Llama 32 vs Qwen3 36 vs gemma 42)

gemma.partition: 3:1 (Hexad+Law+Phi L36 / SelfRef L32, only 1 stride apart)

verdict.reasoning: Llama is dramatic outlier (depth_std 7.88× gemma, 32-layer). gemma's weak decoupling (1 stride, 9.5% depth_range) is consistent with layer_stride=4 sampling noise — distinguishing real decoupling from grid artifact requires finer stride. Mistral and Qwen3 fully coupled (4:0). N=4 too small to distinguish HFD-A (orthogonal axis) vs HFD-B (CMT sub-axis) cleanly: only Llama exhibits strong decoupling, single-backbone observation cannot establish independent axis.
honest_caveats: HFD-C ARTIFACT cannot be ruled out: layer_stride=4 + Llama's 32 layers (vs Qwen3 36 / gemma 42) creates different sampling grids that may bias which layer captures the dominant signal.
N=4 backbones is statistically very small. Single-backbone strong outlier (Llama) cannot empirically establish an axis—it can only flag a candidate.
gemma weak decoupling (4-layer/1-stride apart) is at noise floor of layer_stride=4 grid; could be sampling artifact rather than true biological decoupling.
Family list (Law/Phi/SelfRef/Hexad) and prompts are frozen v10 set; decoupling could be prompt-set-conditional rather than backbone-intrinsic.
axis 95 (CMT) HCMT-B verdict was already PARTIALLY_CONDITIONAL — axis 96 candidate is a refinement of that conditional structure, increasing risk of HFD-B sub-axis interpretation.

next_actions.weakest_link: N=4 backbone sample insufficient + layer_stride=4 sampling artifact possibility
to_promote_HFD-A: (1) Re-scan Llama at layer_stride=1 (32 layers full grid) to confirm Hexad L28 vs L4 partition is not stride-grid artifact
(2) Add 5th backbone (DeepSeek-7b-base, AN11(c) GWT 8/8 registry) to test if decoupling reappears outside Llama family
(3) Add second 32-layer backbone (Mistral-7B-v0.3 already 32L coupled — pick alternate 32L model like CodeLlama-7B) to disentangle layer-count-confounding from architecture-confounding
(4) BBA matrix re-run: compute family-decoupling-axis correlation with existing 6 axes to test orthogonality (axis 95 CMT, axis 8th TRIBE pilot, etc.)
next_actions.to_dismiss_to_HFD-B: Demonstrate full statistical reduction of family-decoupling to CMT family-conditional layer pattern (i.e., decoupling = direct consequence of CMT 4-family finding, no extra variance explained).
next_actions.to_dismiss_to_HFD-C: Show layer_stride=1 re-scan still shows L28 Hexad outlier on Llama, ruling out grid-induced artifact; or alternatively, reproduce decoupling on a 32L non-Llama model to remove the architecture-confound.

BBA_impact_estimate.if_HFD-A_confirmed: BBA matrix expands 6→7, requires re-orthogonality measurement. own#3 (c) 4-axis greedy basis revision possible if axis 96 enters top-4 by independence score.
BBA_impact_estimate.if_HFD-B_confirmed: axis 96 absorbs into axis 95 as conditional sub-finding; no BBA expansion.
BBA_impact_estimate.if_HFD-C_confirmed: axis 96 retracted; remains a methodology lesson (layer_stride sampling).
